mnemonics: SEE: Summarize, Explore, and Evaluate your search results visually.
keyPoints: Visualizations help summarize large sets of results (e.g., with histograms or tag clouds).
They allow users to explore and refine results through interactive elements like sliders and maps.
Visual cues (like relevance scores or thumbnail previews) help users quickly evaluate document usefulness.
commonMistakes: Overloading the interface with too many complex visualizations, causing confusion.
Using inappropriate visualizations that don't match the data type (e.g., a pie chart for temporal data).
Prioritizing visual appeal over clarity and functionality, making the interface less usable.
